title: DeviceCapability
description: What the target device can do, advertised on every observation so the cloud frontier loop degrades gracefully (e.g. an XR headset reports supportsCoordinateGesture=false + hasScreenshot=false, so the loop runs tree+intent only). Populated on the phone side by the M1 DeviceCapabilities.detect(); this schema is the wire shape (M0 contract).

formFactor.description: Physical class of the device. Drives capability expectations: phone/tablet/foldable → all paths; xr_headset → node+intent only (no coordinate gesture, capture unconfirmed); glasses → drive the paired phone (compute lives on the phone via Jetpack Projected).

hasScreenshot.description: Whether the device can supply a screenshot in the observation (silent AccessibilityService.takeScreenshot(), API 30+, requires android:canTakeScreenshot). False on XR (headset-view capture unconfirmed) → the loop runs tree-only, never requesting a screenshot.

supportsCoordinateGesture.description: Whether dispatchGesture-based coordinate_tap / coordinate_swipe actuation is meaningful on this device. False on XR (per-panel 3D compositor, no flat framebuffer) → the loop uses element_click (ACTION_CLICK) + intents only; coordinate actions are skipped/logged.

displayId.description: Target display for capture + gesture. 0 = Display.DEFAULT_DISPLAY. Non-zero for DeX / external / multi-display; used by GestureDescription.Builder.setDisplayId() and takeScreenshot(displayId) in the M5 large-screen work.

accessibilityEnabled.description: OPTIONAL (M8.1) — whether the on-device AccessibilityService is enabled. When FALSE (disabled / OS-revoked, e.g. Android Advanced Protection) the SCREEN paths are unavailable — hasScreenshot + supportsCoordinateGesture are also false and the ui_tree is empty — and the device is INTENT-ONLY (a screen action returns an intent_only_mode action_result). Additive + back-compat (absent ⇒ treat as true).

posture.description: OPTIONAL (M5.5) — a FOLDABLE's hinge posture, present only when formFactor is 'foldable'. Populated from Jetpack WindowManager FoldingFeature (FoldingFeatureMonitor); absent on non-foldables. A CHANGE in posture between observations invalidates prior screen coordinates (see observation.posture_changed) → the loop re-observes before its next coordinate action.

state.description: Hinge state: FLAT (fully opened / flat) or HALF_OPENED (bent, e.g. tabletop / book mode).

orientation.description: Hinge axis when known (vertical hinge = book fold; horizontal hinge = laptop/flip fold).
